subject: [PATCH v2] btrfs: replace writeback inhibition xarray with a fixed inline buffer

reviews.author: Filipe Manana
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the Reviewed-by tag was incorrectly placed and provided a link to the Btrfs developer's FAQ for correct ordering.

The reviewer pointed out that comments about structure members should be in a line above the member, starting with a capitalized word and ending with punctuation, citing documentation guidelines.
reviews.raw_body:
Reviewed-by: Filipe Manana <fdmanana@suse.com>

Some minor things to fixup below.

First, the Reviewed-by tag is in the wrong location, see:

https://btrfs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dev/Developer-s-FAQ.html#ordering

---

Comments about structure members should be always be in a line above
the member, and the first word should be capitalized, ending the
sentence with punctuation.
You did that well for inhibited_eb, but not the the other 2 members.

See:

https://btrfs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dev/Development-notes.html#comments

Thanks.

2026-07-02.patch_summary: This patch replaces a dynamic xarray used to track inhibited extent buffers in Btrfs with a fixed inline buffer, managed by a CLOCK eviction policy. The xarray was causing two issues: a write_iops regression due to the overhead of storing and retrieving data on every copy-on-write (COW) operation, and an unbounded cleanup walk that could lead to multi-second stalls. The new approach uses a small fixed array to track the working set of buffers revisited across COW operations, with a CLOCK eviction policy to reclaim write-once buffers first. This change is expected to improve performance by reducing the overhead of tracking inhibited buffers and preventing long-running cleanup walks.
